title: V-Model artifact — shared definitions
description: Reusable $defs referenced by envelope.schema.json and every per-artifact schema. Single source of truth for scope-path regex, artifact-type discriminator, lifecycle status, ISO date shape, version numbers, retrofit recovery status, and loose artifact-ID stubs. Per-artifact schemas tighten id / status further.

kebab_segment.description: One kebab-case segment: lowercase letters / digits, single hyphens separating runs.

scope_path.description: Slash-separated kebab-case segments naming a scope in the tree. Empty string denotes the root scope.

artifact_type.description: Discriminator for the six V-Model artifact types.

lifecycle_status.description: Universal lifecycle for every artifact. draft = being written or under review, not yet in force. active = current in-force version. superseded = replaced by a newer artifact (see supersedes / superseded_by links).

iso_date.description: Last-modified date in ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). No time component.

version.description: Monotonic integer version. Bumped on material revisions. Git carries the history; this field is a stable ordinal that can be referenced from tests, reviews, and traceability.

recovery_status_scalar.description: Retrofit provenance for a whole artifact or a single field.

recovery_status_map.description: Per-field / per-section recovery status. Keys name fields or sections within the artifact.

recovery_status.description: Retrofit provenance marker. Scalar form applies the status to the whole artifact; object form targets named fields or sections. Per-artifact schemas may tighten further — notably the no-fabrication rule bans 'reconstructed' on human-only fields.

artifact_id.description: Generic artifact ID stub. Per-artifact schemas constrain this with the type-specific pattern (PB; REQ-*; ARCH-*; DD-*; TS-*; ADR-*).

artifact_id_ref.description: Reference to another artifact by ID. Same shape as artifact_id; context distinguishes authoring from referencing.
